compute.cost_note: spaCy en_core_web_sm POS/dep + lemma; CPU, no API costs.

do_not_use_when: You want any authorship, AI-provenance, or writing-quality inference (this surface refuses it)
You want a literary-quality judgment (close and distant narration are both valid choices)
Target is under 1500 words (the length floor)

optional: --verb-lexicon to override the perception/cognition verb set

artifacts: stdout markdown report (default), --json envelope, or --out PATH

purpose: Descriptive narratorial-distance / free-indirect-discourse (FID) profile for developmental editing of literary fiction: per-window pronoun anchoring, perception/cognition-verb density, deictic anchoring (proximal vs. distal), evaluative-adjective density, a heuristic FID score, an overall close/distant distribution, and a distance trajectory across document position. Adjacent to pov_voice_profile but NOT a voice-identity, authorship, AI, or quality instrument — measurements only (no band, no verdict); FID detection is heuristic, not a parse of literary intent.
use_when: You want to see where narration sits close to vs. distant from a character's consciousness, and how that moves across the text
You're developmentally editing literary fiction and want an FID / narratorial-distance trajectory
